Title: Markland Locks & Dam Mowing Services Contract
Description: This Request for Quote (RFQ) is for the Contractor shall furnish all necessary management, personnel, materials, supplies, tools, equipment, fuel, and vehicles, except as otherwise provided herein, required to perform mowing services at the Markland Locks and Dam project recreation and operational areas, as specified and in strict accordance with all Terms, Conditions, General, Specific and Technical Provisions, Drawings, Attachments, Exhibits, etc., contained herein or incorporated by reference. Quotes are due on 7 April 2026 at 1:00 PM Eastern Time. NOTE: THIS RFQ IS 100% SET ASIDE FOR SMALL BUSINESS. The NAICS code for this procurement is 561730. The size standard is $9.5M. The solicitation will be available on the web only. Telephone and Fax requests for this solicitation will not be honored. Project files are Portable Document Format (PDF) files and can view, navigated, or printed using Adobe Acrobat Reader. To download the solicitation for this project, contractors are required to register at the Federal Business Opportunities. Amendments will be available from the beta.sam.gov website by download only. Prior to submission of quotes all contractors must have an active registration in the System for Award Management (SAM) database. To register or update information go to http://www.SAM.gov. Questions may be addressed to justin.m.mcpherson@usace.army.mil. This announcement serves as the advance notice. ***Amendment 0001*** The attached Summary of Changes provides a list of provisions and clauses that have been updated to comply with the Revolutionary FAR Overhaul (RFO). This amendment to the solicitation also clarifies that this is for mowing services only. Any reference to spraying services in the extended CLIN descriptions has been removed. The Statement of Work remains unchanged. The solicitation closing date is hereby extended to 09 April 2026 at 1 PM EDT. Offerors must acknowledge this amendment by signing the SF 30 along with the initial Request for Quote (RFQ) by signing the SF 1449 when submitting their quote. All other terms and conditions in the solicitation remain unchanged. ***Amendment 0001***

Title: Parata ATP
Awardee: PARATA SYSTEMS LLC Durham NC 27703 USA
Description: The requirement will be sole sourced to Parata Systems, LLC., 106 Roache Drive, Durham, NC 27037. Parata Systems, LLC. for the provision of the Parata ATP2-E480 Automatic Tablet Packager, including proprietary software, installation, integration, training, and preventive maintenance services required to support VA pharmacy automation operations. Parata Systems, LLC. is the only source for required proprietary software, installation, integration, training, and preventive maintenance services. The vendor has proprietary software that is not offered or authorized to third party vendors. A sole source letter was received from Parata Systems service department which states that Parata doesn’t authorize third-party vendors to service Parata equipment due to proprietary hardware and software, to include software updates. This contract also requires remote access to perform diagnostics for expedited service/repair of the ATV2, which is a highly specialized system. All software patches and a manufacturer’s maintenance service plan. All warranties and guarantees on the system are voided if an unauthorized third-party vendor is engaged.

Title: FY26 Tug Boat Rental
PopStreetAddress: Delivery to be made in accordance with the attached PWS.
Awardee: FIO LLC 16322 BARSTOW RD East Moline IL 61244 USA
Description: This posting is a solicitation to award a contract for a tug boat rental for the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers, Buffalo District. The boat will be primarily used on Lake Erie from Buffalo, NY to Toledo, OH. The North American Industrial Classification System Code (NAICS) for this effort is 532411 - Commercial Air, Rail, and Water Transportation Equipment Rental and Leasing. This will be a 100% Small Business Set-Aside competitive procurement. Please be advised that this requirement may be delayed, revised, or cancelled at any time before award. SOLICITATION WEBSITE: The official solicitation and any subsequent amendments or updates, when posted, will be available free of charge by electronic posting only and may be found at Procurement Integrated Enterprise Environment (PIEE), https://piee.eb.mil/. Paper copies of the solicitation will not be issued. Telephone and Fax requests for this solicitation will not be honored. Project files are Portable Document Format (PDF) files and can be viewed, using Adobe Acrobat Reader. To download the solicitation for this project, contractors are required to register at the Procurement Integrated Enterprise Environment (PIEE) website at https://piee.eb.mil/. Solicitation Amendments, if/when issued, will be posted to the above referenced website for electronic downloading. This will be the only method of distributing the solicitation and any amendments by the Government prior to closing. It is the Offerors responsibility to monitor the website for any amendments or changes to the solicitation. REGISTRATIONS: Offerors shall have and shall maintain an active registration in the following database: System for Award Management (SAM): Offerors shall have and shall maintain an active registration in the SAM database at http://www.sam.gov to be eligible for a Government contract award. If the Offeror is a Joint Venture (JV), the JV entity shall have valid SAM registration in the SAM database representing the JV as one business/firm/entity. If at the time of award an Offeror is not actively and successfully registered in the SAM database, the Government reserves the right to award to the next prospective Offeror. INQUIRIES: Technical inquiries and questions relating to this solicitation are to be submitted via Bidder Inquiry in ProjNet at http://www.ProjNet.org/ProjNet. As noted, offerors shall NOT submit their proposals via ProjNet. Offerors shall submit their proposals in accordance with the provisions stated in the solicitation. To submit and review bid inquiry items, bidders will need to be a current registered user or self-register into system. The Solicitation Number is: W912P426QA002 The Bidder Inquiry Key is: 2PU72K-VA2S77. For detailed instructions on accessing Projnet, see Addendum to 52.212-1, Instructions to Offerors - Commercial Products and Commercial Services in the attached solicitation. Amendment 1: Amendment 1 changes the structure of the CLINs to include a line item for mobilization/demobilization.

Title: Remote Health Readiness Program 4 (RHRP-4)
Awardee: OptumServe Health Services, Inc. La Crosse WI 54601 USA
Description: OptumServe Health Services, Inc., La Crosse, Wisconsin, is awarded the Remote Health Reserve Program-4th Generation (RHRP-4) Support Services for $1,068,444,680.13 for medical, dental, and behavioral health services in the form of assessments, physical exams, laboratory services, diagnostics, audiology services, optometry services, immunizations, dental exams, and dental treatment in the United States (the 50 States and the District of Columbia), the Commonwealths of Puerto Rico and the Northern Mariana Islands, the territories of American Samoa, Guam, and the U.S. Virgin Islands. The total maximum dollar value (program ceiling) for all task orders placed under this Indefinite-Delivery/Indefinite-Quantity (ID/IQ) contract (HT001126DE001) is $1,610,000,000. The guaranteed minimum dollar value for the entire contract period of performance is $11,000,000. The first task order is for Transition-in effort in the amount of $22,823,948.94 O&M funding, at the execution of the basic contract award. Solicitation HT001125R0020 received 4 timely offers. The total period of performance for the RHRP-4 requirement consists of not to exceed 9-month transition plus a 3–6-month base period (depending on the length of the transition-in period), and nine 12-month option years, for a total of ten years if all options are exercised from Mar. 31, 2026 through Mar. 30, 2036. The contract was procured under full and open competition with small business participation subcontracting goals of 30% of the total contract value. The Defense Health Agency Professional Services Contracting Division, Falls Church, Virginia, is the contracting activity.
